status_detail: Engine Phase3-B31 last-line parser fix DEMONSTRABLY WORKED for header preservation: Access-Control-Expose-Headers (last header) is now present with value '*'. Response headers count: 5 (vs prior runs that lost the trailing header). EVENTS DELIVERED: 2 SSE events parsed (IN_PROGRESS + CHAT_COMPLETE), a BREAKTHROUGH vs #607/#612 which delivered 0 events. HOWEVER underlying H2 stream-close root cause from #607/#612 is unchanged: status (failed) net::ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED 200 (OK). Headers tab shows Status Code: 200 OK, Network column shows (failed). Engine still RSTs/closes the H2 stream after emitting headers+events instead of sending a clean END_STREAM. No UI render: DOM check confirms neither '보안' nor '일시적인 오류' present; no chat bubble added; user's own prompt also never rendered as a user bubble; complete UX silence.
eventstream_first_event_summary: {"event":"IN_PROGRESS","chunkMessage":{"id":"MESSAGE-f5bf2917-30d..."}} followed by {"event":"CHAT_COMPLETE","replyMessage":{"id":"MESSAGE-f5bf2917-3..."}} -- both share same MESSAGE-f5bf2917 prefix (single AI reply: chunk + completion frame)
progress_vs_607_612: better -- engine fix preserved Access-Control-Expose-Headers (last header) AND now actually delivers 2 SSE events before close (prior cycles 0 events)
cross_corroborating_signal: chat-model-configs and feature-flags/web-purchase on the same H2 connection ALSO failed with ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR, suggesting connection-level (not per-stream) framing health issue.
root_cause_hypothesis: Phase3-B31 last-line parser fix correctly resolved the trailing-header drop, BUT underlying H2 stream-close root cause from #607/#612 is unchanged. h2_end_stream=2 likely still maps to RST_STREAM rather than a final DATA+END_STREAM, so Chrome marks the whole transaction as ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED -> fetch reader rejects -> EventSource onerror fires -> zeta-web has no code path to render the (now-discarded) partial text -> silent failure.
recommendation_for_dev: Engine v3+: replace h2_end_stream=2 (which maps to RST_STREAM) with explicit final DATA frame containing END_STREAM flag. Reference Go nethttp2 server.go:1100 for clean-close pattern.
Verify connection-level health: chat-model-configs ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR on the SAME H2 connection suggests this is not per-stream but connection-wide framing corruption.
Compare with chatgpt H2 close behavior (working): does chatgpt send DATA+END_STREAM atomic, or RST_STREAM, or something else?
After clean END_STREAM, expected: events still 2, but status 200 OK (no failed marker), Chrome fetch resolves, zeta-web renders the warning text from CHAT_COMPLETE event.
errors_observed: DOM: no '보안' text, no '일시적인 오류' popup, no chat bubble
notes: Cycle 95 R-10 -- zeta verify post engine last-line parser fix. PARTIAL with significant progress: header preservation verified (5 headers including Expose-Headers), 2 events delivered (vs 0 before). Subagent 9min, exceeded 8min hard timeout but returned valid verdict with breakthrough finding.
